statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 19 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, as oil prices surge 50% following Middle East supply disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as oil stocks remain well below comfortable levels across multiple countries. While EU-wide averages show petrol at 103.8 days and diesel at 96.2 days - both above the mandatory 90-day minimum - jet fuel stocks at 90.1 days hover dangerously close to the threshold. Most concerning, 19 of 27 EU countries are experiencing critical fuel shortages in at least one category, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg showing critical levels across all three fuel types.

The situation reflects the ongoing impact of Middle East supply disruptions that began in late February, which have driven Brent crude oil prices up 50.1% from $72.48 to $108.81 per barrel. Several countries have already released strategic reserves to manage the crisis, contributing to the current low stock levels. Romania presents the most acute concern with only 33.2 days of petrol and 32.4 days of diesel - far below the 90-day safety net.

Jet fuel emerges as the most vulnerable fuel type, with 20 of 27 countries showing critical or warning status. This poses particular risks to aviation and economic connectivity. While some countries like Estonia and Slovenia maintain strong reserves exceeding 365 days for jet fuel, the majority are operating with insufficient buffers. Price pressures are already evident, with petrol averaging €1.777 per liter and diesel at €1.913 per liter across the EU.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries have critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg critical across all fuel types
Jet fuel stocks are most vulnerable at 90.1 days EU average, with 20 of 27 countries below safe levels
Oil prices have surged 50.1% since late February Middle East disruptions began, from $72.48 to $108.81 per barrel
Romania faces the most acute shortage with only 33.2 days petrol and 32.4 days diesel reserves
Despite strategic reserve releases, multiple countries including major economies like Germany, France, and Italy maintain critical status in key fuel categories
